Class UserSessionService

java.lang.Object
com.sebastiandorata.musicdashboard.service.UserSessionService
@Service public class UserSessionService extends Object
Holds the currently authenticated user for the lifetime of the application.

Session tracking: how the timer works

Storage:

  1. JavaFxApplication.start() calls recordAppStart() once as the very first thing after the Spring context is ready. This stamps appStart with the true "app open" time.
  2. When the user logs in, setCurrentUser(User) stamps sessionStart with the login time. Kept separately in case future features need to distinguish "since launch" from "since login".

Retrieval:

  1. DailyListeningStatsService.buildStatSnapshot() calls getSessionDurationSeconds().
  2. getSessionDurationSeconds() returns now - appStart, so the stat card reflects total time the app has been open, including time spent on the login screen.
  3. The value flows into StatSnapshot.todayAvgSessionSeconds, which StatCardsViewModel formats and hands to the stat card label.

Limitations:

  • All state is in-memory and lost on restart. Only the current run is tracked.

  • Constructor Details

    • UserSessionService

      public UserSessionService()

  • Method Details

    • recordAppStart

      public void recordAppStart()
      Records the moment the JavaFX application window first opens. Called exactly once from JavaFxApplication.start(). Subsequent calls are no-ops so a re-shown auth screen cannot reset the clock.

    • setCurrentUser

      public void setCurrentUser(User user)
      Sets the authenticated user and records the login time.
      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user; must not be null

    • getCurrentUserId

      public Long getCurrentUserId()
      Returns the current user's ID, or null if no user is logged in. Time Complexity: O(1)

    • getSessionDurationSeconds

      public int getSessionDurationSeconds()
      Returns the number of seconds elapsed since the application launched.

      Uses appStart (set in JavaFxApplication.start()) rather than sessionStart (set at login), so the timer reflects total time the app has been open, including time spent on the login screen.

      Falls back to sessionStart if recordAppStart() was never called, and returns 0 if neither has been set. Time Complexity: O(1)

      Returns:
      elapsed seconds since app launch, or 0 if unavailable
